items.task: Extend pre-commit hook to block protected paths on all branches
items.context: Hook only checks protected paths when `current_branch == "main"`. Workers can commit `src/` on `worker-N` branch, which then gets merged into main by supervisor fallback.
items.done_when: Protected-path check fires for all branches when `AGENT_WORKER=1`, not just main.

items.task: Iterative-review secret scanning should cover full file contents
items.context: Current secret scanning only checks the diff. Reviewers have full file-read access, so secrets in unchanged hunks of reviewed files are still exposed.
items.done_when: Scan full contents of all files in review scope (not just diff hunks) before each round.

items.task: Tribune Slack token exposure in config/docs
items.context: Gemini-side Slack MCP config and Tribune design docs contain literal Slack token values instead of env var indirection. Risk of leak into logs/context/history. Issue
items.done_when: All Slack tokens use env var indirection; literal values scrubbed from config, docs, and git history.

items.task: Tribune review handoff integration gaps (4 bugs)
items.context: AGENT-033 shipped the Tribune role but left 4 integration bugs: (1) worker prompt doesn't override `waiting_human` for draft-requiring tasks, (2) Slack post failure after approval doesn't requeue, (3)
items.done_when: All 4 handoff gaps resolved; Tribune review → Slack delivery path reliable end-to-end.

items.task: Slack `conversations_add_message` returns phantom `ts`
items.context: Returned `ts` can't be retrieved as a real thread root via `conversations.replies`. Affects maintenance task lifecycle (first post establishes `thread_ts`). Issue
items.done_when: Maintenance thread creation uses a Slack API method that returns a retrievable `ts`, or validates the returned `ts` before relying on it.

items.task: `_swap_model_in_cmd` doesn't handle `--model` long form
items.context: Helper only recognizes `-m`, not `--model` or `--model=...`. If `TRIBUNE_CMD` uses long form, fallback inserts a duplicate flag. Found during FIX-006 iterative review (M-3-1).
items.done_when: `_swap_model_in_cmd` and `_extract_model` handle `-m`, `--model`, and `--model=` forms.

items.task: Worker worktree shared symlinks break normal git flow
items.context: Symlinked `.agent/` and `projects/` in worktrees break `git status`, `git add`, and submodule commands. Workers need index-level workarounds. Failure modes 1-2 fixed; remaining friction documented. Is
items.done_when: Workers can use standard git commands on shared paths without index-level workarounds, or the workaround is fully automated.

items.task: Gemini MCP Slack error noise during iterative-review
items.context: Gemini CLI tries to connect all configured MCP servers at startup regardless of `--approval-mode plan`. Slack MCP fails to connect, producing error noise in reviewer stderr. Non-fatal but noisy.
items.done_when: Gemini reviewer dispatch suppresses or avoids Slack MCP connection, either via config override or `--no-mcp` flag.

items.task: Thread length cap: finish current thread and start a new one when thread exceeds ~50 messages
items.context: Long-running tasks accumulate large threads that degrade Slack UX and balloon prompt context.
items.done_when: When thread message count exceeds threshold (configurable, default ~50), worker finishes current thread with a link-forward message and initiates a new thread; task JSON tracks multiple `thread_ts` en

items.task: Define supervisor→worker thread presentation contract
items.context: Supervisor passes raw `mention_text` and thread snapshots to the worker with no formal schema for how thread context is structured. Worker-facing rules about message format (removed from AGENTS.md as
items.done_when: Supervisor renders thread context into a documented, versioned schema; worker contract references the schema; edge cases (attachments, reactions, edits) are handled explicitly.

items.task: Multi-worker project collaboration: coordination when multiple workers touch the same project
items.context: With 8 concurrent workers, multiple may be dispatched to tasks in the same project submodule. Currently no coordination — workers would clobber each other's changes in the shared symlinked project dir
items.done_when: Workers dispatched to the same project coordinate via a locking/queuing mechanism; concurrent same-project work either serializes or partitions cleanly; merge conflicts from parallel project edits are

items.task: Persistent thread sessions: session resume + mid-execution thread awareness. Plan
items.context: Phase 0 spike completed (2026-03-21): `codex exec resume <SESSION_ID> -` works in piped subprocess mode, session IDs parseable from startup header, full context retention confirmed. Next: Phase 1 impl
items.done_when: Phase 1: drop `--ephemeral`, capture session IDs, re-dispatch via `codex exec resume <ID>` with slot affinity. Phase 2: workers check `conversations_replies` at phase boundaries (behavioral contract).

items.task: Google Drive backup for agent system via Google Drive for Desktop
items.context: No cloud backup for runtime artifacts (~2.2GB). Machine loss = total state loss. Git now tracks codebase only; `.gitignore` cleaned to remove artifact paths.
items.done_when: Google Drive for Desktop installed, `~/Research/`, `~/.claude/`, and `~/.codex/` syncing to Murphy's Google Drive; verified on drive.google.com.

items.task: Rethink behavioral contract structure across three roles
items.context: Currently: `AGENTS.md` for Worker (Codex), `CLAUDE.md` for Developer (Claude), `GEMINI.md` for Tribune (Gemini). Each CLI auto-loads its own doc, but the separation needs design review — shared vs. ro
items.done_when: Coherent multi-role contract architecture; clear boundaries between shared system docs and role-specific behavioral contracts; no duplication of rules across docs.

items.task: User-scoped credential/asset registry
items.context: No durable index for user-scoped credentials and shared assets (accounts, API keys location, shared resources). Workers repeatedly search for the same info. Issue
items.done_when: Structured per-user or system-wide registry of credential locations and external assets; workers can look up "where is X?" without re-discovery.

items.task: Multi-role round-table discussion skill
items.context: Generalization of AGENT-035 iterative review to collaborative deliberation. Deferred: speculative, depends on AGENT-035 maturity in production. Existing loop mode covers deliberation use cases. Revisi
items.done_when: A Claude Code slash command for multi-perspective design discussions.

items.task: Clean up dispatch prompt: remove unnecessary metadata from thread context
items.context: `_task_json_to_text()` formats each message as `[2026-03-21 07:01 UTC (1774076482.886199) | user: U08HX3SH33R | role: human]` — the raw Slack `ts` in parentheses and raw `user_id` are unnecessary for
items.done_when: Thread context rendered with human-readable timestamps and resolved usernames only; raw Slack `ts` and `user_id` stripped or moved to non-prompt metadata.

items.task: Tool-aware worker idle watchdog
items.context: AGENT-028's session-log staleness watchdog can false-kill workers during legitimate long tool calls (e.g., 30-60 min Athena consults). Plan
items.done_when: Watchdog distinguishes idle workers from workers blocked on long tool calls; no false kills during legitimate Athena/GPU operations.

items.task: Overleaf git sync skill
items.context: Steady-state workflow for syncing Overleaf projects via local git clone. Plan, bootstrap plan
items.done_when: Codex skill automates Overleaf pull/push/conflict resolution, including initial project bootstrap.

items.task: Tribune default model: switch to `gemini-3.1-pro`
items.context: `TRIBUNE_CMD` defaults to `gemini-2.5-pro`. User requested switching to `gemini-3.1-pro`. Plan
items.done_when: `TRIBUNE_CMD` defaults to `gemini-3.1-pro`; fallback chain updated accordingly.

Roadmap page on agent website with dispatch-on-click. Separate `roadmap/index.html` linked from dashboard launchpad. ROADMAP parser, priority-colored cards, Slack webhook dispatch (`!developer` comman

User profiling: per-user `.agent/user_profiles/<user_id>.md` files injected into worker prompts via `{{USER_PROFILE}}`. Stub creation on first interaction + startup backfill. Robust frontmatter parsin

Multi-role iterative code review skill: `/iterative-review` Claude Code slash command. Tribune (Gemini) + Murphy (Codex) as adversarial reviewers with distinct lenses. JSON schema, workflow doc, desig

Tribune role: independent quality reviewer via Gemini CLI. Dynamic maintenance phases (`TRIBUNE_MAINT_ROUNDS`), post-dispatch quality review (`TRIBUNE_MAX_REVIEW_ROUNDS`), contract-based draft mode, 1
Reorganize `.agent/` directory into 5 categories (archive, memory, runtime, skills, tasks/projects). 24 source files updated, 335 tests pass. Dashboard monitor hardcoded paths fixed. Submodule branch
Ecosystem scouting: daily developer review step tracks online agent system developments via WebSearch/WebFetch. Living project registry + append-only findings log. Maintenance scheduling changed from

Block symlink-mode (120000) submodule commits: pre-commit hook rejects symlink entries on `projects/*`/`mcp/*` paths; git-workflow.md documents correct `update-index --cacheinfo` procedure for worktre

Closed as not-a-real-issue: supervisor-side ff-only merge in `_reconcile_slot` is already serialized by the single-threaded main loop; git's `index.lock` prevents concurrent index corruption; existing

JSON integrity validation: `scripts/validate_agent_state.py` checks parsability, schema compliance, cross-references, and Slack conversation completeness. Incremental by default. Developer review chec
Fix parallel dispatch worktree isolation: early `git clean -fd` before checkout in reset; pre-commit hook blocks direct-to-main commits; merge instructions rewritten. 2 new tests, 335 pass.
Worker hang detection: session log staleness watchdog (`WORKER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SEC=900`), reduced `COLLECT_JOIN_TIMEOUT` from 2h to 10min. 4 new tests, 333 pass.
Shell-job MVP: async job execution with durable records, CAS acknowledgements, ShellAdapter integration, startup recovery. All 4 phases complete.

Dashboard brutalist cyberpunk redesign: pure black, zero radius, thick borders, offset shadows, chromatic aberration title. Showcase link as animated pill (holographic shimmer border, glitch jitter, r

Persist consult (Athena) conversation history: three-layer persistence (global archive `.agent/consult_history/{task_id}.jsonl`, per-project aggregate `.agent/projects/{slug}.consult.jsonl`, behaviora

Fix consult MCP ChatGPT UI selector migration: `main article` → `SEL_ASSISTANT_MSG`, updated completion buttons, diagnostic logging in response detector, developer review validation step with `check_s
Visualization system: `visualization-task` skill packaged with Mermaid-first workflow, provider routing, QA checklist, and delivery protocol. External MCP providers deferred.
Move 13 Codex skills to `.agent/skills/` with per-skill symlinks; version-controlled, Codex discovery via symlinks. Consult/GPU skill packages deferred — workflow docs cover them.
Pin `agent-monitor-web` submodule at checkpoints; set `ignore = all` in `.gitmodules` to hide continuous publisher drift.
Remove legacy `first_task_objective_candidate()` and `mention_text_override` path — all tasks use structured JSON since AGENT-008.
Remove redundant supervisor done notification (`_notify_task_done`). Delivery guard unchanged — sound and aligned with prompt language.
Reduce no-op state rewrites: skip `save_state()` in `ensure_state_schema`, `prune_finished_tasks`, `prune_stale_waiting_human_tasks` when unchanged.
(Absorbed into AGENT-007) Optimize `ensure_state_schema()` to skip rewrite when state is already current.
Consolidate `_ts_sort_key()` and `ts_to_int()` into single canonical utility.
Make Python runtime explicit: `from __future__ import annotations` in all modules, auto-detect python3.11 in launch script.
Enforce completion-gate semantics (`high/moderate/low`) with explicit downgrade reasons.
Per-task outcomes at `.agent/outcomes/<mention_ts>.json` (absorbed into multi-agent dispatch).
Stream worker logs to file (absorbed into multi-agent dispatch).
Fast polling: `POLL_INTERVAL=5` for ~5s mention detection.
Remove legacy text snapshot; structured JSON approach is sole path.
Migrate `.agent/tasks/` from Markdown to per-task JSON with thread history.
Atomic select+claim under single lock (absorbed into multi-agent dispatch).

visibility.audience: Anyone with the GitHub Pages URL.
